title: Here’s how much a remote per diem worker makes north of Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A senior project manager living in Hamilton-Wenham earns $27,000 annually, working per diem at $45 per hour.
After transitioning from full-time to per diem work remotely, Francine relies on her salary, pension, and 403(b) for financial stability.
Her major monthly expense has shifted from commuting costs to healthcare since enrolling in Medicare.

title: NTSB: Flight instructor in fatal Mass. plane crash had ‘do not fly’ meds in system
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The NTSB reported flight instructor William Hampton, involved in a 2024 crash, had medications on the FAA's 'do not fly' list.
Hampton's system contained alprazolam, amitriptyline, zolpidem, and citalopram, potentially impairing his ability to fly.
The NTSB determined Hampton's failure to maintain control after a stall led to the fatal 2024 Massachusetts plane crash.

title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Bagpiper Nate Silva performed at Harry's Bar and Grill in Boston on St. Patrick's Day.
Silva has played at over 30 locations in Boston during his busy St. Patrick's Day schedule.
Silva, active on social as @passionpiping, performs at about 100 events annually across the country.

title: 50 ways to make the most of spring in Massachusetts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closed after over 20 years; a new restaurant will open in April.
Boston hotels are increasing prices for FIFA World Cup; Airbnb offers $750 bonus to new hosts.
JetBlue canceled the seasonal Boston-Asheville route; impacted customers to receive full refunds.

title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady appeared on 'The Tonight Show' discussing his flag football debut and life post-retirement with host Jimmy Fallon.
Brady reflected on his new perspective in football from his FOX broadcasting role, appreciating the young generation of players.
The Fanatics Flag Football Classic event, featuring Brady and Rob Gronkowski, was moved to Los Angeles from Riyadh due to conflicts.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Approximately 30 TSA employees have quit at Logan Airport amid a continuing government shutdown affecting paychecks.
Logan Airport has implemented new camera technology to estimate real wait times for security lines at Terminal B.
Despite staffing issues, Logan Airport security lines remain manageable, unlike other major airports experiencing longer delays.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lianes Brandao, 28, died after a fight and hit-and-run in Brockton identified by authorities.
Police found Brandao at 4 a.m. Sunday and he was later pronounced dead at a Boston hospital.
The suspected vehicle, a gray 2013 Jeep Compass, was found, and the driver interviewed by police.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, a 36-year-old from Newton, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Haberek, who battled triple-negative breast cancer during pregnancy, is fundraising for the Ellie Fund.
The Ellie Fund supported Haberek during her treatment, and she aims to give back through the marathon.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, a Korean restaurant, opened in Allston, offering fish sets, banchan, and a blend of mountain and ocean flavors.
Upcoming openings in the Korean trio include Bombbada in July, providing late-night dining, and Jinjee in mid-April, focusing on BBQ.
The restaurant trio occupies a renovated space formerly known as Tavern in the Square, enhancing Allston's authentic Korean food scene.

title: The 5 most exciting books to read this spring — what’s on your list?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rosie Hewlett's 'Sweetbitter Song' retells Melantho and Penelope's story from Homer's 'Odyssey,' releasing on March 17, 2026.
Tara Menon's 'Under Water,' explores friendship loss and spans natural disasters, available from March 17, 2026.
Caro Claire Burke's 'Yesteryear' merges past and present with a tradition-focused psychological thriller, releasing on April 7, 2026.
